content: Your Z2 time was only 18% of total run duration. During aerobic base phase, the target is >70% Z2. This session counts as INVALID for aerobic development.
rationale: The physiological adaptation from aerobic base training — increased mitochondrial density, fat oxidation efficiency, and capillary density — requires sustained effort in Z2. Time spent in Z3/Z4 stimulates a different energy system and does not substitute for Z2 volume.
metadata: {"category": "Zone Quality", "verdict_rationale": "Only 18% Z2 time against a 70%+ target. 65% of the run was spent in Z3/Z4, indicating the effort was too intense for an aerobic base session."}

content: Heart rate drifted from 148 bpm (km 0-4) to 165 bpm (km 8-12) at similar pace. This 17 bpm drift at constant pace is a clear sign the aerobic system was stressed beyond its current capacity.
rationale: Cardiac drift at constant pace indicates the cardiovascular system is working harder over time to maintain the same output — a hallmark of running above the aerobic threshold. True Z2 training should show minimal drift (<5-8 bpm over a standard session).
metadata: {"category": "Cardiac Efficiency", "verdict_rationale": "17 bpm cardiac drift over 11.8 km at constant pace. Threshold exceeded from the first kilometre."}

content: Next session must be at true Z2 effort. Use heart rate as your only guide: if HR exceeds your Z2 ceiling, slow down — walk if necessary. Target pace is secondary to zone.
rationale: After a session where the aerobic system was over-stressed, the following workout should reinforce proper zone discipline. Running by heart rate (not pace) in the next session will train the habit of effort-based pacing.
metadata: {"category": "Recovery & Planning", "verdict_rationale": "Session invalidated for aerobic base purpose. Corrective easy run prescribed."}
